front: Painting: over 80 paintings of guards and dogs torturing blindfolded prisoners; inspired by Seymour Hersh article; donated to Berkeley
Painting: requested to be exhibited at all times as a 'permanent accusation'
Painting: a giant man on rooftops riddled with bullets, eyes closed, holding a gun up to the sky
Painting: parody of the Mona Lisa at 'Age 12' with bloated face filling a quarter of canvas; created before the 1963 US tour
Painting: self-portrait peeks from behind easel alluding to Las Meninas; a riff on Goya's The Family of Carlos IV
Artist: parodied Raphael's Portrait of Pope Leo X (Pope with magnifying glass and silver bell)
Artist: created the Dove of Peace sculpture from the remains of Bird after it was blown up by a bomb
Artist: credited his style to an early still life of a mandolin with an extremely small central sound hole
Artist: adapted signature style to sculpture in the 1980s (Maternity, Roman Soldier)
Artist: expelled from Jesuit high school for praising Picasso's Cubism
Artist: studied at the Prado copying Goya paintings; Broadgate Venus stands at Liverpool Street Station
Artist: namesake museum in Bogota's La Candelaria; Botero Plaza in Medellin displays dozens of bronzes
